_description: Rules for XUI event handlers using _on, lifecycle hooks, _xem signals, and data-only nano-command handlers.
_core_rules: Use Xpell/XUI event handler fields only.
Do NOT generate DOM handler attributes such as onclick, onchange, onkeyup, or onsubmit.
Do NOT generate addEventListener inside views.
Prefer JSON nano-commands over JS functions.
Do NOT generate JS functions unless explicitly requested.
Persisted or agent-generated handlers must be data-only.
Use _on for DOM-style object events such as click, change, input, keyup, submit.
Use lifecycle hooks for runtime lifecycle behavior.
Use _on_frame for frame-driven behavior instead of timers.
Use _xem or the fire nano-command for signals, not for storing state.
Events coordinate behavior; events are not state.
_priority_rules: When the requested action is to run/trigger/execute a FlowManager flow, do NOT use _on.click.fire. Use root-level _flow from xui-flow-trigger.
For button actions that are not FlowManager flow triggers, use _on.click with a data-only handler.
For input changes, use _on.change or _on.input, not _on_change.
For repeated frame behavior, use _on_frame, not setInterval.
For cross-object or app-level signals, use fire or a module command to xem.
Use command JSON or handler sequences when more than one action is needed.

_global._usage: Use _xem for app/global coordination signals. In persisted/generated JSON prefer command/nano-command forms instead of JS handlers.

_object._usage: Use _on for object events and lifecycle hook fields for lifecycle behavior.

_nano_commands._description: Fire an Xpell/XEM-style signal from an object handler.

_modules._description: Global event coordination module when available.

_ops._description: Fire a named event with explicit payload.

_patterns._description: Button click using _on.click and a data-only nano command for non-flow actions.
Use a sequence for multiple event actions.

_anti_patterns._reason: Do not use DOM handler attributes. Use _on.click.
Persisted or agent-generated handlers must be data-only, not JS functions.
This fires a raw XEM signal. FlowManager flows must use root-level _flow.

_notes: This skill depends on xpell-contract, xpell-core, xui-core, and should defer FlowManager flow triggers to xui-flow-trigger.
_on is the preferred object event map for DOM-style events.
_xem/global events are coordination signals, not state storage.
Use _on_frame only for frame-driven behavior where the runtime supports it.
FlowManager flow execution belongs to xui-flow-trigger and should use root-level _flow.
